detailed_analysis: walker.py is the public orchestrator of the discovery package: it owns DiscoveryWalker, exposing discover() (the per-poll walk) and state_owner_for_path() (a state-lookup helper), while delegating per-cell enumeration, adoption planning, and collision blocking to three mixins (EnumeratorMixin, AdoptionPlannerMixin, CollisionBlockerMixin). The file is small (119 lines, well under the 300-line module limit), tool-agnostic (no concrete tool name appears anywhere — Goal 5 / architecture stable-abstractions rule upheld), and correctly gates participation on tool availability (US-11 AC-4) and supported_customization_types (US-10 AC-3). The dominant structural concern is cooperative multiple inheritance: DiscoveryWalker mixes in three mixins that read self.config / self.agentic_tools / self.tool_status (declared only here) and call state_owner_for_path (declared only here), an implicit, unenforced contract spread across four files. No CRITICAL findings: no hardcoded secrets, no injection, no silent exception swallowing in this file (the broad excepts live in the mixins, not in scope), no mutable defaults, no no-op bodies. The discover() method is borderline on length (~40 effective lines) but stays under the cyclomatic and nesting thresholds. Remaining issues are MINOR/SMELL: config typed dict[str, Any] (primitive obsession, project-wide and documented at architecture 5.5), an untyped tuple return, and the mixin coupling. NFR-15 secret_policy is not evaluated in this file, but discovery is a read/parse boundary whose secret handling is delegated to the IO adapter parse functions; no propagation/egress occurs here, so this is not a walker.py deviation.

dependency_graph_summary.note: Layer-2 use case importing only inward (agentic_tool_spec port, rendering/state gateways, sync_types entities, tool_status sibling use case, config.expand_path utility per documented deviation D-2). Dependency Rule upheld.

findings.variant: Cooperative-inheritance implicit contract / inappropriate intimacy across mixins
findings.problem: The three mixins read self.config, self.agentic_tools, self.tool_status (defined only in this __init__) and call self.state_owner_for_path / self._planned_adoption_targets / the _block_* helpers, but none of these collaboration points is declared as an abstract interface or Protocol. The contract that binds the four files is implicit and unenforced: a mixin could reference a renamed attribute and only fail at runtime. mypy cannot see the cross-mixin calls. This is fragile cooperative inheritance — composition with an explicit collaborator interface would make the seam checkable.

discover() is ~40 effective lines, right at the project's 40-line limit, with a two-level loop plus the post-walk eviction pass (lines 92-93) folded into the same method. Complexity (7) and nesting (3) are within thresholds, so this is a borderline MINOR rather than a true Long Function. The trailing late-block eviction loop is a second distinct responsibility (cleanup of partial inserts) that could be its own helper.

config is a dict[str, Any] with an implicit schema accessed by string keys (e.g. self.config[spec.config_dir_keys[customization_type]] at line 74, and again across the mixins). A typed config object would localise the schema and let mypy catch key/shape drift. This is a project-wide pattern, documented as the use-case<->config seam (architecture 5.5), so it is reported as a SMELL, not a defect.

discover() returns a positional 2-tuple (pairs, blocked_pair_ids). The relationship between the two is load-bearing (blocked ids have already been popped from pairs, per the docstring), and callers must remember which element is which. A small named result (NamedTuple/dataclass) would make the contract self-documenting. Minor; the docstring mitigates it.

notes: Goal 5 / architecture 1 stable-abstractions rule: no concrete tool name (claude/codex/...) appears in walker.py; iteration is over self.agentic_tools.items(). Upheld.
US-11 AC-4 / FR-04: tools whose status is not available are skipped at lines 62 and 65, so an unavailable tool never appears as a removal signal. Upheld.
US-10 AC-3: participation is restricted via sorted(spec.supported_customization_types) and spec.io[customization_type]. Upheld.
Dependency Rule (architecture 3): imports are inward-only (port + gateways + entities + sibling use case + expand_path utility per D-2). Upheld.

NFR-15 / Goal 6: walker.py performs no render/export/import egress; the only parse occurs indirectly via the adoption planner mixin and the IO adapter parse functions. No secret literal is propagated from this file, so the secret_policy egress boundary is not this file's responsibility. Not a deviation.
I-3 archive-before-write (architecture 8): walker.py performs no destructive writes (no write_text/rename/unlink/rmtree). Not applicable. Upheld vacuously.

verdict_summary: Clean, tool-agnostic Layer-2 orchestrator; no CRITICAL/MAJOR findings. Main smell is unenforced cooperative-mixin coupling. code_score 0.75.
